moduleDescription: This module contains support for a rope data type. Ropes can represent very long strings efficiently; in particular, concatenation is done in O(1) instead of O(n). They are essentially concatenation trees that are only flattened when converting to a native Nim string. The empty string is represented by nil. Ropes are immutable and subtrees can be shared without copying. Leaves can be cached for better memory efficiency at the cost of runtime efficiency.

entries.name: Rope
entries.code: Rope {.acyclic.} = ref object
entries.description: A rope data type. The empty rope is represented by nil.

entries.name: len
entries.code:
proc len(a: Rope): int {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1", raises: [], tags: [],
                         forbids: [].}
entries.description: The rope's length.

entries.name: rope
entries.code:
proc rope(s: string = ""): Rope {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1Str", raises: [],
                                  tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts a string to a rope.

entries.name: rope
entries.code:
proc rope(i: BiggestInt): Rope {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1BiggestInt", raises: [],
                                 tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts an int to a rope.

entries.name: rope
entries.code:
proc rope(f: BiggestFloat): Rope {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1BiggestFloat",
                                   raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts a float to a rope.

entries.name: enableCache
entries.code: proc enableCache() {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Enables the caching of leaves. This reduces the memory footprint at the cost of runtime efficiency.

entries.name: disableCache
entries.code: proc disableCache() {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: The cache is discarded and disabled. The GC will reuse its used memory.

entries.name: `&`
entries.code:
proc `&`(a, b: Rope): Rope {.gcsafe, extern: "nroConcRopeRope", raises: [],
                             tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: The concatenation operator for ropes.

entries.name: `&`
entries.code:
proc `&`(a: Rope; b: string): Rope {.gcsafe, extern: "nroConcRopeStr",
                                     raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: The concatenation operator for ropes.

entries.name: `&`
entries.code:
proc `&`(a: string; b: Rope): Rope {.gcsafe, extern: "nroConcStrRope",
                                     raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: The concatenation operator for ropes.

entries.name: `&`
entries.code:
proc `&`(a: openArray[Rope]): Rope {.gcsafe, extern: "nroConcOpenArray",
                                     raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: The concatenation operator for an openArray of ropes.

entries.name: add
entries.code:
proc add(a: var Rope; b: Rope) {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1Rope", raises: [],
                                 tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Adds b to the rope a.

entries.name: add
entries.code:
proc add(a: var Rope; b: string) {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1Str", raises: [],
                                   tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Adds b to the rope a.

entries.code:
proc `[]`(r: Rope; i: int): char {.gcsafe, extern: "nroCharAt", raises: [],
                                   tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the character at position i in the rope r. This is quite expensive! Worst-case: O(n). If i >= r.len or i < 0, \0 is returned.

entries.name: leaves
entries.code: iterator leaves(r: Rope): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over any leaf string in the rope r.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items(r: Rope): char {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over any character in the rope r.

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; r: Rope) {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1", raises: [IOError],
                               tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Writes a rope to a file.

entries.name: write
entries.description: Writes a rope to a stream.

entries.code:
proc `$`(r: Rope): string {.gcsafe, extern: "nroToString", raises: [], tags: [],
                            forbids: [].}
entries.description: Converts a rope back to a string.

entries.code:
proc `%`(frmt: string; args: openArray[Rope]): Rope {.gcsafe,
    extern: "nroFormat", raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: % substitution operator for ropes. Does not support the $identifier nor ${identifier} notations.

entries.name: addf
entries.code:
proc addf(c: var Rope; frmt: string; args: openArray[Rope]) {.gcsafe,
    extern: "nro$1", raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Shortcut for add(c, frmt % args).

entries.name: equalsFile
entries.code:
proc equalsFile(r: Rope; f: File): bool {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1File",
    raises: [IOError], tags: [ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true if the contents of the file f equal r.

entries.name: equalsFile
entries.code:
proc equalsFile(r: Rope; filename: string): bool {.gcsafe, extern: "nro$1Str",
    raises: [IOError], tags: [ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true if the contents of the file f equal r. If f does not exist, false is returned.
